# The Regulator's Apprentice

Boxwell returned through the low fog of autumn, cedar case strapped to his back.
A clockmaker's reputation travels faster than a clockmaker, and the village of
Harrow Fen had been waiting for him. He repaired the church regulator before
noon, patient as ever, refusing payment beyond a warm meal.

When a boy asked how long he had been a clockmaker, Boxwell smiled and said the
cedar tool case had belonged to his teacher, and that he had carried it longer
than the boy had been alive.
